approved_claims.notes: This is a regional estimate and should not be generalized to all of Asia without additional evidence.
approved_claims.text: Authoritative WHO reporting located here is consistent with the WHO South-East Asia Region having a large share of the world's adolescent tobacco users; the cited figure is about 11 million users aged 13-15 years, or about 30% of the global total, using surveys conducted during 2012-2022 and applied to 2021 populations.

approved_claims.notes: Figure text indicates the estimate is based on surveys from 2012-2022 and population application to 2021.
approved_claims.text: The same WHO evidence is consistent with an average current tobacco-use prevalence around 11% among adolescents aged 13-15 years in the WHO South-East Asia Region.

approved_claims.notes: These are older regional estimates and may not reflect current prevalence.
approved_claims.text: Older CDC/WHO GYTS reporting suggests that, in South-East Asia, any tobacco use among students aged 13-15 years was about 12.9% and cigarette smoking was about 4.3% in surveys conducted during 1999-2005.

approved_claims.notes: This supports the presence of public health information infrastructure, but not direct prevalence of teen exposure to information.
approved_claims.text: Publicly accessible surveillance infrastructure on youth tobacco exists through CDC's GTSSData system, which CDC describes as covering more than 20 tobacco-data topics across four survey families.

final_format: Use the CSV artifact as a compact evidence table; treat findings as regional tobacco-use prevalence evidence and survey-coverage metadata rather than a direct measure of 'public health info prevalence.'
